reviews.review_description:
All around, this game is awful. The physics engine barely works, animations are terrible and the ai is nothing special. 
Enemies do not react to your attacks and the pre programmed same attacks get very repetitive quickly. Enemies deaths are quite bad, with heads and hands popping off randomly or enemies not reacting in the slightest to damage, no in-between 
The weapons do not behave realistically and are plagued with glitches such as getting stuck in other enemies weapons, doing no damage or being unwieldy. Two handed weapons like long swords are literally unusable as they have practically no movement. Axes and maces are so heavy and sluggish they are beyond useless. This means that only single handed SWORDS are usable. 
The boss fights are horribly designed , they all kill in one hit, have insane pools of health and often can't be damaged except for one or two weak spots. They take ages to kill, are boring repetitive and frustrating. This sort of artificial difficulty is quite terrible imo and the devs should have tried to make an actually hard boss through new skills and attacks rather than cranking it's health and damage to silly levels.
However, I find the single handed combat such has with sabres or rapiers can be fun, especially against the pirates which is the single reason I give this review 2 stars rather than 0.
Overall, it's a waste of money.

reviews.review_title: Best Sword Combat ai on the quest but...
reviews.review_description:
Really impressed with the AI in this game. The combat feels as real as it could especially when you have no armor on. But I will say it's quite easy so best to set ai to maximum difficulty in arena modes. Even so the combat is enjoyable so props to the developers on that. 

The only reason I'm not giving this game 5 stars is that I want to see a cooperative mode for the arena. I don't think the game needs PVP as there are many other options on the quest for PVP combat but Cooperative survival modes would be perfect.

It went from a 3 to 4 stars. (12-15-25)

I'm not an arcadey type of gamer on VR. I like VR games that can help train you in real life. And I think this game scratches that itch. Sort of.

If you play this game like you are actually training for a sword fight, then I can see some benefits to it. However, there's game-y aspects to this game and some bugs that hold it back.

Now this game does offer a light RPG leveling up system. And I do find it kind of fun because you can create a certain archetype character. Like either a really fast character. Or a really strong character. However, if you just want to play the game and try to cheese it, all you really need to do is just focus on the strength and the speed and the health. 

This game can be fun, but I still find it lacking something. Now I do find that the swinging mechanics is a little wobbly and janky. Now recently started playing again and they feel less janky. And I noticed some clip through. Leaving me vulnerable for a game over. But it's not as bad as it used to.

I complained about the maces previously but I played just recently (12-15-25) and now the maces feel viable. And I can swing with one hand. It has the weighty wobble to it but it's not as bad as it used to feel.

I also played with two handed weapons and they feel a lot better.

One handing swords is the path of least resistance. Because you can combo it with a shield. It's definitely more meta than a dagger/sword combo. I still need to try that combo again.

 I will say the bow and arrow mechanic is fun! I think it's great but I'm sure it could it better.

I think the special weapons and special armor types are a fun edition. But for some of the armours, I feel like there should be a special effect for each armour. Not fun effects but effects for balance. Example: the archer armour gives more arrows. Knight armour gives more strength. Again I would like the mechanics balanced for realism rather than quirky fun.

I find that the enemies all fight the same. The AI can be easily manipulated. And sometimes you have to use crappy tactics like swing spamming the legs.

There really isn't much to do either than just arena fighting. They try to say that there's a story mode, but really it's just fighting a bunch of waves. And then you move on to the next round. There's really no story or anything else to it.

The leveling up system ceases after a few levels. Like all you have to do is really just focus on strength and speed and health and that's really it. Everything else is just redundant. But I also think it takes away from the realism that this game is marketed as. I think the slow motion perfect block mechanic actually worsens my experience.

Money gets a little useless after a while too. So there's really no reward system that makes me want to grind more.

The thing is this game is trying to be like an RPG arcade and a realistic fencing game at the same time. And I honestly don't think that's really working. You really should focus on the realistic fighting part because we already have arcade like melee games. I bought this game because this game is the most realistic sword game out there.

I think Hellish Quart is a great game to try to follow in. Because they use physics and real fighting techniques from other cultures with unique animations.

I really would like to see the game feel a little bit more fluid. A little bit more balance on the weapons and armours. The enemies all feel the same. And just give a little bit more with the game modes rather than just an arena. I do like the arena part of it but don't include a story mode when it's just another arena challenge.
One of the best vr game I’ve played love it

reviews.review_title: This has ruined blade and sorcery for me
reviews.review_description:
I don’t do HEMA myself but I’m decently knowledgeable and know the basics about it. I love HEMA and didn’t like the fantasy sword combat in most games because it’s clunky and boring. I’ve only played this game for about 10 minutes and love it. The graphics are nothing special, but the mechanics are top tier. It’s so rewarding closing an opponents guard with a Cutlass and jabbing them in the face, or having a perfect parry and riposte. The amour works well on both you and opponents along with the sword physics. The AI can get repetitive but mostly can be hard to take out and counter. I do also see the AI do real tactics and manage there distancing pretty well and  will kill you with with ease if you charge in blindly 

9/10, can get better but is really good, if you do HEMA and want a fun past time, give this a try
Really good game I hope the developers add multiplayer mode to fight real people aswell

I've been looking for a game with good wordplay and so far this is the best one. The Ai is reactive and fun to go against. I beat the first "chapter" without realizing I could upgrade or change my gear but that was without the experimental Ai or armor physics so it's not too difficult but it still held a challenge. I beat the main game in the span of an hour but it's replaybility isn't bad at all, especially if you want a game that focuses mostly on realistic combat. Not much story, not really much moving around. It's pretty stationary as you fight through waves of 1-2 enemies in 4 different maps. Definitely better than blade and sorcery as far as combat goes but I do wish there was modding compatibility.

Dev response on reddit

"We pushed 15 FREE major updates for Swordsman in under 4 years and gave away over $50,000 worth of prizes to the community. How do you get to the idea that we abandoned? Considering standard in VR we over delivered to our community. 

It was cheaper and we could have gotten funding for it, but we wanted to try more dynamic combat, we did and we learned a lot. 

Why would we ever take advice or feedback from an individual who doesn't understand our history, our reasoning and our objectives and on top of everything is just rude?"

To those who just play a game for 10 min, then
 leave a review is not a gamer. I first gave this game a three star because of the slow swinging and short life two hits your done. But when you level up your player, put gear on him. I level up the speed(Agility) of swinging and now it's almost on point with my swinging. Play the game level up then leave a review. I almost didn't buy the game because of the last review. But I'm a gamer I need to test it.
Why can't I refund it that is stupid

I kinda like this game. The weapons and armor choices are abundant, the level system in on point. The boss fights are challenging and interesting. The kraken scares the crap out of me.
The controls are clunky and make me feel sluggish in movement. The opponents don't have very many different moves and the different areas are just the same fighting styles with opponents in different costume. I think I would enjoy it more if I were moving through a level or area instead of being at a stationary place with enemies spawning in one after another. Throwing a weapon feels way off with tragectory. I don't know how my sword is able to pierce through steel armor and when my opponent kicks and I slice the leg it never registers or dismembers the leg. I think with some minor tweeks this game could be a lot more fun.

And after a few updates this game has really improved. Devs are committed to this one. It's not a 5 star game yet imo but with a little fine tuning it can get there. I'm updating to 4 stars. Great job improving quality of life for this title. The AI update made the opponents seem more fluid and realistic. The environments have become more immersive and the combat no longer feels clunky. Throwing weapons still feels off but in fairness your not really sposed to throw your weapons in this game. It's a good title and a solid 4 stars.

reviews.review_title: Very annoying issues, mainly on realism
reviews.review_description:
Armor is basically worthless. Royal armor has a def of 20 and fully covers the body, yet a normal sword slicing at it, or a zombie’s jaw, can still kill you with ease. Enabling armor physics is only for the enemy, in which most of them still have weakspots you can exploit, that they’ll rarely ever try to defend. If you play without armor physics for enemies, then you can bash all bosses with the starter sword without a care in the world and win because they will be stun locked. If you play with armor physics for enemies, then only you are using armor that does nothing. I’d only give the boss with the fire sword a pass on this cause his sword is just lava
Blocking can feel pointless most of the time. Enemy’s swords like to just ignore yours and damage you, even going through your sword or shield sometimes 
Also, although pretty uncommon, because you’re stunning a boss, or interrupting their animation, they can attack or use their weapon ability (mainly clause and sometimes Hel) while looking like they are stunned/blocking/doing a different attack and get free, unblockable attack on you. 
The enemy waves are very repetitive. There is little to no difference between lvl 1 knights and lvl 9 knights except gold armor knights spawn (this is almost the same for about every faction you fight), which, they have the same vulnerabilities as the regular knights
Armorless bosses, like Clause and Commander James Falcon (offbrand Captain Jack sparrow) shouldn’t just have a mountain of HP, it makes fighting them a drag. If they are going to fight armorless, then they should be easy to kill/wound when you actually hit them. Make them harder to hit via good swordsmanship, not turn a sword game into a game of wack-a-mole + RNG sword blocking for 1-3 minutes

reviews.review_title: most realistic sword play I've seen.
reviews.review_description:
I have fenced with saber and am a new student with HEMA, learning long sword and arming sword. I was impressed with the stance and footwork of the AI, similar to what I'm learning. The AI are fast and don't telegraph their intensions like other games. They don't, however, thrust with their swords (at least not as far as I've seen). The fighting is more difficult than other games - more challenging; nice job. I look forward to future updates.
For the Devs: here are a couple books to examine. I hope these will help you to develop the fighting style of the AI. These are two books written by masters of the arts of combat centuries ago.
1.
The Art of Combat: A German Martial Arts Treatise of 1570 Paperback – October 30, 2020
2. 
The Flower of Battle: MS M 383

I love this game the only problem is the graphics. while good, games like battle talent, blade and sorcery, and hellsplit have better graphics. But this game gives the realist most realistic fighting!

Swordsman VR is one heck of an experience. 
When it comes to VR games, I'm extremely picky. Typically, refunding most games I've bought to try something else. I even returned this a few days ago, to try Battle Talent. Let me tell you, this is where it's at. Simple, yet complex.
Realistic, tactical and to the point.
The campaign is fun, quite short but that's where the other game modes come in clutch.
In between every couple campaign missions, I run an arena mode. Here, you select a difficulty and amount of waves, and earn gold for weapons/armor, and skill points. (Earnable in all modes)
A must have VR game for sure. I even bought weighted gloves just to simulate the weight of the weapons which really can turn this into a workout! Great game. Thanks Devs!
I love the gore and the campaign it's fun

It's not perfect, but it's really good! I like the way it feels when I play. You can tell the devs truly put the time and effort in! The sword play is a little more edgy compared to some of the other sword fighting games. The NPC's put up a good fight, but you can find gaps in their defense. So far, it has the best sword play, in my opinion, so I'm giving it 5 stars.
It’s ok
Honestly, the game is alright. They don't update it enough. It has a lot of potential. Do I recommend it? No, it gets boring after a while.
Not worth the money, and it would be fine, IF I COULD RETURN IT
